content:
I found a half-broken fade on my own portfolio and went down a rabbit hole — one tiny setting separates polished from irritating.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

🔓 Not a Medium member? Read this free here.
You know the effect. You scroll down a nicely designed site, and each section gently fades in as it enters the screen. A little rise, a little fade — it feels alive, considered, expensive.
I love this effect. I use it on my own portfolio.
While debugging mine, I ran into a question I’d never thought about: When someone scrolls back up… should the animation play again?
My instinct was “sure, why not — more animation, more delight.” Turns out the common advice is the opposite: play it once, then never again. I went down a rabbit hole to understand why, and the reasons turned out to be a nice little tour of UX ideas that apply way beyond scroll effects. Here’s what I found, in my own words.
1. Content is the meal. Animation is the garnish.
Here’s the mental model that made it click for me:
The first time someone reaches a section, they’re discovering it. A soft fade-in makes that…
